explainability.findings: A Databricks blog post touches upon challenges related to AI governance and explainability, specifically noting issues arising from siloed platforms.
explainability.path_to_improvement: Document how explanations are provided to users affected by AI decisions.

relevant_sources.summary: This Databricks blog post provides evidence for explainability, fairness, governance, privacy, and transparency in responsible AI. It highlights mechanisms like the AI Gateway for centralized governance, credential management, and rate limits, as well as tools for post-launch model assessment and sensitive information detection, supporting governance and privacy. The post also discusses the importance of avoiding bias for fairness and mentions the AI Security Framework for mitigating risks, contributing to governance and security. Furthermore, it touches upon challenges in AI governance and explainability due to siloed platforms, while also emphasizing the need for operationalizing transparency and trust.
relevant_sources.title: Databricks Blog: Responsible AI with the Databricks Data Intelligence Platform

external_accountability.path_to_improvement: Obtain external validation of AI practices or require vendor certifications.

fairness.findings: A Databricks blog post discusses the importance of avoiding bias to ensure fairness in AI.
fairness.path_to_improvement: Publish bias testing results, outcome monitoring, or vendor fairness certifications.

governance.findings: The "2023 Growth & Impact Report" mentions the planned addition of an AI-driven analytics platform, ECOLAB3D. A Databricks blog post highlights mechanisms such as the AI Gateway for centralized governance, credential management, and rate limits. It also mentions tools for post-launch model assessment and the AI Security Framework for risk mitigation, while touching upon challenges in AI governance due to siloed platforms.
governance.path_to_improvement: Name an AI governance body with defined mandate covering all AI use.

relevant_sources.summary: The "2023 Growth & Impact Report" provides evidence for the **governance** pillar of responsible AI. This company-owned report mentions the planned addition of an AI-driven analytics platform, ECOLAB3D, to improve water recycling performance. The inclusion of this AI solution suggests a process for its implementation and integration into their services, which aligns with governance practices.
relevant_sources.title: 2023 Growth & Impact Report

oversight.path_to_improvement: Document human review processes for AI-assisted decisions (built or vendor AI).

privacy.findings: A Databricks blog post highlights mechanisms such as credential management and rate limits that support privacy. It also references tools for sensitive information detection.
privacy.path_to_improvement: Publish AI-specific privacy impact assessments or data handling procedures.

transparency.path_to_improvement: Publish detailed documentation (model cards, system specs) for AI systems deployed.

summary.overall_findings: Drawing from 12 publicly available sources, Ecolab's published materials address 4 of 7 evaluated responsible AI pillars. These disclosures include discussions on operationalizing transparency and avoiding bias for fairness. For privacy, materials highlight tools for sensitive information detection, and governance documentation references mechanisms such as the AI Gateway for centralized governance. No qualifying public evidence was found for explainability, oversight, and 1 additional pillar.
